QF.id: Income, Residential Status, Five Heads of Income_12

question.text: Under which head of income is commission earned by a sub-broker from a stock broking firm classified?

answer.text: Income from Salary, since it is received regularly from a firm
Income from Other Sources, as it is a residual income
Profits and Gains of Business or Profession (PGBP)
Capital Gains, since it arises from securities transactions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Commission earned by a sub-broker is income from carrying on the business/profession of broking. There is no employer-employee relationship (sub-broker is independent), so it is not Salary. It is not capital gains (no transfer of capital asset) or residual income. It falls squarely under PGBP. Note: importantly, commission/brokerage earners CANNOT opt for presumptive taxation under sections 44AD/44ADA. **The Trap:** Some candidates classify any "commission" as salary or other sources. The key distinction is the relationship — employer-employee = salary; independent business = PGBP. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) tests broker/sub-broker regulations; Series X-B tests the tax classification of their income. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Sub-brokers and distributors earning commission must maintain books of accounts and file under PGBP — they cannot take the presumptive taxation shortcut under 44AD, which explicitly excludes commission/brokerage income.
